settings.title: How We Hand a Project Back to You
settings.excerpt: A good launch is not the end of our job, it is the moment your team takes the wheel without fear.
settings.body:
People sometimes ask what happens after we ship. The honest answer is that the last week of every build is about you, not us.

We record a short walkthrough of how to edit each page type, write a plain language guide to the parts that are not obvious, and sit down with whoever will own the site after we leave.

The last week, step by step

By the final week the site is built and tested, so the work shifts entirely to making sure your team can run it. We block out real time for this rather than squeezing it in around the launch.

  • A recorded walkthrough for each page type you will actually edit.
  • A short written guide to the few things that are not self-explanatory.
  • A live session where your team makes a real edit while we watch.

Our developers, Mara and Devin, treat the handoff as a feature. If your team cannot confidently update the homepage on their own, we have not finished.

A tip before launch day

Pick the person who will own the site early, and bring them into the last two weeks rather than introducing them at the end. The handoff lands far better when the owner has already seen how the pages were built.

We stay reachable after launch, of course, but the goal is that you rarely need us. A site you cannot change yourself is a site that slowly goes stale, and we would rather hand you something you can keep alive.
